titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/245

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=218 status=live modified=2022-11-01 13:07:36.847303+00 | translation moa=219 status=live modified=2022-11-01 13:07:38.025367+00

p.cl: Willelmus de Veteri ponte omnibus hominibus & amicis suis . & omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis clericis & laicis . Salutem .

Sciant presentes .
& futuri  me in liberam & perpetuam elemosinam dedisse & hac carta confirmasse decimas meas de terra mea de Oggel
fast . & de venatione mea . & decimas hominum illorum qui super terram illam habitauerint deo & ecclesie sancte crucis de
edenesburc . & canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus ita liberas & quietas sicut aliqua elemosina liberior in tota terra
regis Scocie tenetur uel habetur .

T . Forno capellano . Radulfo capellano . Ricardo de veteri ponte . Rogero quirem . Reginaldo
de puntiv . Rogero de Careden . Godwino de careden . Adam de hele . Teobaldo pistore . Willelmo de butemund .
William de Vieuxpont to all men and freinds of his own, and to all sons of Holy Mother Church, to clerks and laymen, Greeting:

Let those present and those to come know me in free and perpetual alms to have given and by this charter to have confirmed my own teinds from my own land of Ogilface, and of my game, and the teinds of those men who might reside upon that land, to God and to the church of Holyrood of Edinburgh, and to the canons there serving God, so free and quit as any alms is in the whole land of the king of Scotland freely held or had.
Witness Forn the chaplain, Ralph the chaplain, Richard de Vieuxpont, Roger Quirem, Reginald de Poinz, Roger of Carriden, Godwin of Carriden, Adam of Elie, Theobald Fisher, William Buteman.
